title: [PrettifyVerilog?] MulAddRecFN_postMul in FPU.fir has weird redundant statements
body:
I'm not sure what is going wrong here, probably something in PrettifyVerilog?  This is on FPU.fir from firrtl testsuite:

```
$ firtool -verilog FPU.fir 
...
module MulAddRecFN_postMul(     // FPU.fir:4855:10
...
  wire [74:0] _T_16 = {_T_14, _T_15, io_fromPreMul_bit0AlignedNegSigC}; // Cat.scala:30:58
...
  wire [74:0] _T_42 = ~_T_16;   // MulAddRecFN.scala:257:23
  wire [74:0] _T_43 = ~_T_16;   // MulAddRecFN.scala:257:23
  wire [74:0] _T_44 = ~_T_16;   // MulAddRecFN.scala:257:23
  wire [74:0] _T_45 = ~_T_16;   // MulAddRecFN.scala:257:23

  wire [42:0] _T_48 = _T_46 ? (io_fromPreMul_isCDominant ? {1'h0, _T_39} : _T_40 ? (_T_41 ? {_T_44[27:1], 16'h0}
                : {1'h0, _T_43[42:1]}) : _T_41 ? {10'h0, _T_42[49:18], |_T_34} : {_T_45[11:1], 32'h0}) :
                {1'h0, io_fromPreMul_isCDominant ? _T_39 : _T_40 ? (_T_41 ? {io_mulAddResult[25:0], _T_13 ?
                16'hFFFF : 16'h0} : io_mulAddResult[41:0]) : _T_41 ? {8'h0, _T_14[1:0],
                io_mulAddResult[47:17], _T_13 ? ~(|_T_34) : |_T_31} : {io_mulAddResult[9:0], _T_13 ?
                32'hFFFFFFFF : 32'h0}}; // Bitwise.scala:71:12, Cat.scala:30:58, CircuitMath.scala:37:22, MulAddRecFN.scala:207:46, :255:57, :262:62, :308:23, 
```

PrettifyVerilog generally duplicates `~` unary operators to make avoid spilled temps in the generated code, but some expressions must be spilled - specifically those subject to extracts.  We shouldn't duplicate in this case, because it ends up with silly stuff like the above _T_42/_T_43/_T_44/_T_45 lines.
